AN EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 6 MILES WEST OF OWENSVILLE AND TRAVELED EAST FOR 22 MILES TO ONE HALF MILE EAST OF SOMERVILLE BY 204 AM. PEAK WIND WAS 105 MPH...AND THE PATH WIDTH WAS 175 YARDS. NUMEROUS HOMES SUSTAINED MAINLY SHINGLE DAMAGE. SEVERAL GARAGES OR OUTBUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED OR DESTROYED. NUMEROUS BARNS WERE HEAVILY DAMAGED. NUMEROUS TREES WERE UPROOTED AND SEVERAL POWER LINES WERE DOWNED.

AN EF2 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 2 MILES SOUTHEAST OF PRINCETON AND TRAVELLED EAST FOR 19 AND A QUARTER MILES TO 2 MILES WEST OF VELPEN BY 209 AM. PEAK WIND WAS 115 MPH. AVERAGE PATH WIDTH WAS 300 YARDS...BUT IT COMBINED WITH STRAIGHT-LINE WINDS AT ONE POINT TO CREATE A MAX WIDTH OF ONE THIRD MILE. THE MOST INTENSE DAMAGE OCCURRED IN OAKLAND CITY AND 2 MILES EAST OF ARTHUR. NUMEROUS HOMES WERE DAMAGED. 2 HOMES WERE TOTAL LOSSES. SEVERAL HUNDRED TREES UPROOTED OR SNAPPED. NUMEROUS POWER LINES DOWNED.
MULTIPLE HOMES DAMAGED NORTH AND EAST OF THE COMMUNITY.

*** 2 INJ *** ONE STEEL BUILDING DESTROYED. ENTIRE WALLS DEMOLISHED ON OLDER 2 STORY BRICK BUSINESS BLDGS. APPROXIMATELY 30 LARGE TREES UPROOTED AND AROUND A HUNDRED SMALLER TREES BROKEN OFF OR UPROOTED. ROOF TOPS INCLUDING DECKING PARTIALLY OR COMPLETELY TORN OFF 20 HOMES AND BUSINESSES. DEBRIS FROM THE BRICK BLDGS BLOCKED US HWY 51 AND SECONDARY ROADS.
